Joy Hill
Joy Hill, by Wolfhertz ©
Allegiance House Lannister
Culture Westermen
Born 288 AC[1]
Father Gerion Lannister
Mother Briony
Books
  • The World of Ice & Fire (appendix)
  • A Game of Thrones (appendix)
  • A Clash of Kings (appendix)
  • A Storm of Swords (mentioned)
  • A Feast for Crows (mentioned)
  • A Dance with Dragons (appendix)

Joy Hill is the ten-year-old bastard daughter of Gerion Lannister.[2]

Contents

  • 1 Character
  • 2 History
  • 3 Recent Events
    • 3.1 A Storm of Swords
    • 3.2 A Feast for Crows
  • 4 Quotes
  • 5 Family
  • 6 References

Character[ ]

According to her cousin Jaime, Joy is a sweet child, but a lonely one.[3]

History[ ]

Gerion Lannister fathered Joy on Briony, a common woman.[4] Gerion was lost at sea nearly a decade ago,[5] when Joy was two or three years old.

Recent Events[ ]

A Storm of Swords[ ]

As part of the pact between Lord Tywin Lannister and Lord Walder Frey concerning the betrayal of Robb Stark in the Red Wedding, Joy is to wed a natural son of Walder from House Frey once she is older.[6]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

After the siege of Riverrun, Lady Sybell Spicer mentions to Ser Jaime Lannister that his late father, Tywin, had promised a bride from Casterly Rock for her eldest son, Ser Raynald Westerling. Because Sybell mentions "joy", Jaime thinks Tywin intended for Joy Hill to marry Raynald. Sybell is angered at the idea of her son marrying a bastard.[3] According to a semi-canon source, this is a misunderstanding on Jaime's part, as he did not know of Tywin's earlier plans with Joy and House Frey.[7]

Quotes[ ]

Sybell: Your lord father said that Raynald should have joy of him, if all went as we hoped.

Jaime: Joy is my late uncle Gerion's natural daughter. A betrothal can be arranged, if that is your wish, but any marriage will need to wait. Joy was nine or ten when last I saw her.
Sybell: His natural daughter? You want a Westerling to wed a bastard?

Jaime: No more than I want Joy to marry the son of some scheming turncloak bitch. She deserves better.[3]

—Sybell Spicer and Jaime Lannister
